description_long: This object is a highly detailed, pink and white lotus flower with multiple layers of elongated, pointed petals radiating outward from a central core. The petals are arranged in a whorled pattern, overlapping each other and creating a full, voluminous bloom. Each petal displays a gradient of color, with deeper pink hues at the base fading to lighter pink and white toward the tips, and subtle striping along their length. The flower lacks a visible stem or leaves, focusing attention on the intricate petal structure. The overall form is symmetrical and balanced, with petals curving gracefully outward and upward. The surface of the petals appears smooth and slightly glossy, suggesting a delicate, almost waxy texture.
description: A pink and white lotus flower with layered, pointed petals arranged in a whorled, symmetrical pattern. The petals are smooth, slightly glossy, and display a gradient of color from deep pink to white.
description_view_0: This view shows the flower from a slightly elevated angle, highlighting the central core and the layered, outward-curving petals.
description_view_1: This angle emphasizes the left side of the flower, revealing the overlapping arrangement and the sharp tips of the petals.
description_view_2: This perspective displays the right side, showcasing the symmetry and the way the petals fan out from the center.
description_view_3: This view presents the flower from a slightly different rotation, focusing on the rear petals and their layered, whorled structure.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a whorled, radial configuration with multiple overlapping layers, creating a symmetrical and voluminous bloom. Each layer of petals is offset from the previous, giving a spiral-like effect, and the petals are evenly spaced around the central axis.
